name: Bright Data
what_it_does: Commercial web data collection infrastructure providing residential and datacenter proxy networks, a browser API, SERP API, and Web Scraper IDE for agents that need reliable access to geo-restricted or bot-protected web content.
use_cases: Collect competitor pricing data from e-commerce sites that block datacenter IPs using residential proxy rotation
Scrape Google SERP results at scale for a research agent using the dedicated SERP API
Render and extract JavaScript-heavy pages using the managed Scraping Browser without running local Playwright
Bypass geo-restrictions to collect regional content or pricing for a market intelligence agent
Build structured datasets from social media or marketplace platforms using pre-built scraper templates
not_for: Lightweight scraping of bot-friendly public APIs — direct API calls are cheaper and simpler
Real-time sub-second data needs — proxy routing adds latency
Use cases where terms of service compliance and data provenance are critical — review legality per target site
best_when: You need reliable, large-scale web data collection from sites with aggressive bot detection and need managed proxy rotation without building infrastructure.
avoid_when: Your target sites are bot-friendly public resources or you need low-latency real-time data; or your budget does not support usage-based enterprise pricing.

auth.notes: Proxy credentials use username:password format with zone configuration; REST API uses Bearer token auth. Zone-level access controls available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial credits on signup; no ongoing free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-GB for proxy bandwidth; pay-per-request for SERP/Scraping Browser; custom enterprise contracts
pricing.notes: Pricing varies significantly by proxy type: datacenter < ISP < residential. SERP API and Scraping Browser priced separately per request.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent session limits by plan; residential proxy pool is 72M+ IPs with high availability

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Proxy connection errors surface as standard HTTP errors; SERP API errors include descriptive codes but docs are sometimes inconsistent with actual behavior.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Data collection requests are read-only and can be safely retried; webhook delivery has at-least-once semantics.
known_agent_gotchas: Residential proxy sessions expire — agents must handle reconnection logic and not assume session stickiness
Cost overruns can be significant at scale — always set spending caps in the dashboard before running bulk agent jobs
SERP API results may be cached up to a few hours — not suitable for real-time freshness requirements
Proxy zone configuration (country, city, ASN targeting) must be encoded in the proxy username string — easy to misconfigure
Scraping Browser sessions are billed per minute not per request — long-running sessions inflate costs unexpectedly

security_score_components.security_notes: Proxy credentials embedded in connection strings risk exposure in logs — use environment variables and log scrubbing.
